acceptance.proof: Two serialized pinned black-box repeats bind each captured pre-response datagram to exact SHA-256, byte length, minimal parsed structure, finite outcome, and cleanup.
Hash, length, structure, pin, repeat, stability, provenance, limitation, and private-boundary mutations reject without publishing raw sensitive evidence.
Standard, public-boundary, documentation/instruction, and workflow routes pass without runtime, device, or authority widening.

commit_policy: Commit lifecycle separately; all oracle binaries, independently authored private driver, raw datagrams, interface/endpoints, diagnostics, environment, and machine identity remain private; preserve every attempt and version drift.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Bound the exact pinned-official query observation and nonclaims.
Route addressable query evidence separately from runtime support.
Record per-attempt query identity and honest stable/dynamic classification.
Bind black-box oracle, independent parser, private capsule, and public sanitization.
Route focused damaged, Standard, and workflow checks.
Existing compatibility and public/private lane rules cover the observation.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review against the resolved work environment.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Existing observation-before-conformance and authority rules apply.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review socket, protocol, evidence, and authority surfaces.

non_scope: Responding to or replaying the official query, production runtime/API/activation changes, inspecting or copying pylsl/liblsl source, publishing raw sensitive bytes or private endpoints/interface identity/paths/diagnostics/environment, inferring stability across more than two repeats, other groups/ports/address families/interfaces, devices, dependencies, unsafe/FFI, broad compatibility, routing/admission/commands, or Manifold authority.
objective: Observe only the exact IPv4 multicast query datagram emitted by pinned pylsl 1.18.2/liblsl 1.17/protocol 110 before response handling on one caller-explicit active host interface at 239.255.172.215:16571, using two serialized finite black-box repeats and publishing only addressable sanitized hash, length, minimal independently parsed structure, repeat relationship, provenance, and limitations.
outputs: Two private finite capture attempts retaining raw datagrams, interface/endpoints, diagnostics, environment, and every failure outside the repository.
One public-safe fixture with per-attempt SHA-256 and byte length, minimal structural fields, stable-versus-dynamic classification, exact pin identity, private-capsule hashes, and bounded nonclaims.
Focused positive and damaged checker plus documentation, policy, public-boundary, instruction, and workflow routes.
